description: Phishing templates impersonating cloud storage and file sharing services

templates.name: Dropbox File Share
suggested_subject_lines: Someone shared a file with you on Dropbox
Dropbox: New shared folder requires your access
You have a new file share from a colleague
templates.notes: Works well in organizations that rely on Dropbox for external collaboration — the 'someone shared a file' pretext feels routine. Pair it with a believable sender name. Lower-yielding where SSO has replaced standalone Dropbox logins.

templates.name: Google Drive Document Share
suggested_subject_lines: A document has been shared with you on Google Drive
Google Drive: Review requested — Q4 Report.xlsx
Someone shared a file: please review and comment
templates.notes: Highly effective in Google Workspace shops where Drive share notifications are part of the daily workflow. Reference a plausible document name in the subject to boost realism. Less convincing in Microsoft 365-only environments.
